title: Fix flavour mapping (AQI showing as Premium) and unit count off-by-one in reports

requirements.text: Fix the flavour value not being correctly saved or read during CSV/XLS import: when the user selects 'AQI' as the flavour in the CSVImportSection selector before uploading, the stored ReportEntry records must persist 'AQI' as the flavour — not default to or be overwritten by 'Premium'. The flavour shown in the Reports table must exactly match what was selected at upload time.

quotes: Flavour selected as AQI and uploaded but in reports it shows as Premium

acceptanceCriteria: Selecting 'AQI' flavour in CSVImportSection and uploading files results in all imported records storing 'AQI' as the flavour value in the backend.
The Reports table displays 'AQI' in the Flavour column for those records — not 'Premium' or any other value.
Other flavour selections (e.g., 'Premium', 'Others') are still saved and displayed correctly.
Re-uploading with a different flavour selection updates the flavour for affected records correctly.

requirements.text: Fix the unit count mismatch in Reports: when 4 files each representing 1 distinct unit are uploaded (4 files = 4 units), the Reports table footer must show 'Totals (4 units)' and the table must display all 4 rows. Currently only 3 units appear, indicating either an off-by-one error in distinct unit counting, a duplicate detection false positive, or one record being silently dropped during batch upsert.

quotes: Again i had uploaded 4 units, 4 files = 4 units but it shows as 3 units

acceptanceCriteria: Uploading 4 CSV/XLS files each containing a distinct unit ID results in exactly 4 records stored in the backend.
The Reports table for that week displays all 4 unit rows.
The totals row in the Reports table footer reads 'Totals (4 units)'.
No unit record is silently dropped or merged with another during batch upsert.
The distinct unit count logic in reportFilters.ts (or equivalent) does not under-count when all unit IDs are valid and distinct.

constraints: Do not change the overall UI layout or color theme.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Adding new columns or report fields beyond what already exists.
Changing the CSV/XLS parsing logic for any field other than flavour and unit deduplication.
Dashboard layout or KPI card changes.
